zh.lifeSummary: 當Sarah Parker於1583年出生在英國英格蘭維爾爾爾德頓時，她的父親Robert Parker19歲，母親Dorothy Stevens24歲。她於1603年與Reverend John Woodbridge V在英國英格蘭維爾爾爾德頓結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1683年享年100歲。

nameMeanings.name: Parker
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name from Middle English parker ‘park-keeper’ (Old French parquier, parchier), an officer employed to look after deer and other game in a hunting park (see Park 1). This surname is also very common among African Americans. It has also been recorded since medieval times in Ireland.
Americanized form of one or more similar (like-sounding) Jewish names.

variants.name: Parkman
meanings: occupational name from Middle English park ‘enclosure, game preserve, park’ (Old French parc) + man, sometimes for one living at or near such a place but probably more often denoting someone employed to look after after deer and other game in a hunting park; compare Parker .
occupational name denoting the servant (Middle English man) of someone called Park (see Park 2).

variants.name: Parkerson
meanings: English: from the occupational name Parker + patronymic -son.

variants.name: Parkey
meanings: English (Cheshire and Lancashire): dialect pronunciation of Parker . Parker has a long history in Macclesfield, Manchester, and Bridgnorth.

variants.name: Park
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Korean Young, Sang, Sung, Yong, Jung, Jong, Chan, Jae, Jin, Kwang, Kyung, Myung, Chong, Chang, Chung, Byung, Min, Moon, Chul, Hae, Jeong, Dae, Seong, Myong.

English and Scottish: from Middle English and Older Scots parc, parke, perk ‘park, enclosure’ (Old French parc), often referring to the grounds of a manor house or a deer park where the lord hunted. The name was probably synonymous with Parker , denoting an officer who looked after a park for the lord of the manor.
English and Scottish: from a medieval pet form of the personal name   Peter  . Compare   Parkin  .

nameMeanings.name: Sarah
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, borne by the wife of Abraham and mother of Isaac. According to the Book of Genesis, she was originally called Sarai (possibly meaning ‘contentious’ in Hebrew), but had her name changed by God to the more auspicious Sarah ‘princess’ in token of a greater blessing (Genesis 17:15, ‘And God said unto Abraham, As for Sarai thy wife, thou shalt not call her name Sarai, but Sarah shall her name be’). This has been one of the most enduringly popular girls' names.

variants.name: Sara
meanings: Variant of   Sarah .  This is the form used in the Greek of the New Testament (Hebrews 11:11).

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Sarah Parker was born in 1583, in Cholderton, Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Robert Parker, was 19 and her mother, Dorothy Stevens, was 24. She married Reverend John Woodbridge V in 1603, in Cholderton, Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 3 daughters. She died in 1683, at the age of 100.

ko.lifeSummary: Sarah Parker(이)가 1583년 영국 잉글랜드 윌츠히르 컬더턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Robert Parker(은)는 19세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Dorothy Stevens(은)는 24세였다. 그녀는 1603년 영국 잉글랜드 윌츠히르 컬더턴에서 Reverend John Woodbridge V(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1683년 100세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sarah Parker nasceu em 1583, em Cholderton, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Robert Parker, tinha 19 anos e sua mãe, Dorothy Stevens, tinha 24 anos. Ela casou-se com Reverend John Woodbridge V em 1603, em Cholderton, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1683, com 100 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sarah Parker родилась в 1583 г. в местности чолдиртон, уилцхир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Robert Parker было 19 лет, а матери, которую звали Dorothy Stevens, 24. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Reverend John Woodbridge V в 1603 г. в местности чолдиртон, уилцхир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 3 дочери. Она умерла в 1683 г. в возрасте 100 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sarah Parker nació en 1583, en Cholderton, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Robert Parker, tenía 19 años y su mamá, Dorothy Stevens, tenía 24 años. Se casó con Reverend John Woodbridge V en 1603, en Cholderton, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió en 1683, a la edad de 100 años.
